---
title: "Lab 2 — USV PID Tuning"
subtitle: "Week 7 · {{< var quarter >}}"
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Status:** in progress through Week 7. This week is data collection; analysis and performance characterization happen in Week 8.
:::

## Objectives

Tune the autopilot's closed-loop control to maximize speed and yaw-rate tracking performance. By the end of this week each team has step-response data for **three experiments × two channels = six datasets**.

| Experiment | What changes | What stays the same |
|---|---|---|
| **A — Baseline** | Lab preset configuration (rate limiters off, FF off, throttle baseline off); ArduRover stock PID gains | Other settings unchanged |
| **B — Feedforward** | Add feedforward gain to both speed and yaw-rate control loops.  Gain values from our open-loop system identification lab. | Stock PID gains |
| **C — Tuned** | Tune PID gains for "best" response | Feedforward kept on |

For each experiment, both the **speed (surge/throttle)** loop and the **yaw-rate (steering/rudder)** loop are exercised with manual step inputs, and the resulting closed-loop responses are logged and quality-checked.

**This week's deliverable:** clean step-response logs for all six cases, with a brief quality-check note per dataset.

**Next week:** process the data with `closedloop_stepinfo.m` to quantify rise time, settling time, overshoot, and steady-state error — and use those metrics to compare the three experiments.

## Architecture overview

The lab tunes the two innermost feedback loops in ArduRover's ACRO mode:

- **Speed (surge) loop** — PID under `ATC_SPEED_*`, target from RC throttle scaled by `CRUISE_SPEED`, measurement from GPS.
- **Yaw-rate loop** — PID under `ATC_STR_RAT_*`, target from RC steering scaled by `ACRO_TURN_RATE`, measurement from EKF/IMU.

Block diagram, parameter list, and logging channels are in **[Architecture details](architecture.qmd)**.

## Procedure overview

_Working draft — see **[Procedure details](procedure.qmd)**._

At a high level:

1. Confirm preset parameters and the experiment-specific tuning state on the boat.
2. Run a series of throttle and steering step inputs in ACRO mode.
3. Pull the `.BIN` log, convert with `bin2mat.py`, and inspect with `proc_closedloop_response.m`.
4. Verify the data passes the quality check before returning to the dock.

## End state — what "done" looks like this week

For each of the six datasets, you should be able to plot — using [`closedloop_stepinfo.m`](https://github.com/bsb808/introduction-to-feedback-control/blob/main/book/w07_lab_usv_pid/code/closedloop_stepinfo.m) — a step-response figure that shows:

- **Setpoint** (commanded step) overlaid on **measured response**
- **Control effort** (normalized throttle or rudder) on a linked time axis
- **PID internals** (`PIDT_*` for speed, `PIDS_*` for yaw rate) showing target, actual, P/I/D/FF terms, and total output

If those plots look clean — clear step, response settles, no clipping, no logging gaps — the dataset is good. If anything looks off, plan to recollect before leaving the field.

## Materials

- Lab chapter PDF (build pending) — `book/w07_lab_usv_pid/chapter/lab_usv_pid.pdf`
- ArduPilot Rover doc references — see [`ardupilot_references.md`](https://github.com/bsb808/introduction-to-feedback-control/blob/main/book/w07_lab_usv_pid/refs/ardupilot_references.md)
- MATLAB scripts — `book/w07_lab_usv_pid/code/`
  - [`proc_closedloop_response.m`](https://github.com/bsb808/introduction-to-feedback-control/blob/main/book/w07_lab_usv_pid/code/proc_closedloop_response.m) — visual inspection
  - [`closedloop_stepinfo.m`](https://github.com/bsb808/introduction-to-feedback-control/blob/main/book/w07_lab_usv_pid/code/closedloop_stepinfo.m) — step-response metrics
- Log conversion — [`bin2mat.py`](https://github.com/bsb808/introduction-to-feedback-control/blob/main/utils/ardu_utils/bin2mat.py)
